_meta.notes: Subverted-default race. The genre says humans are 'balanced and adaptable jacks-of-all-trades'. Here, humans are brittle specialists: each is born into a single craft, cannot meaningfully switch, and dies young (40-50). Their craft dies with them, so apprenticeships start at age 7 and last seven years; the next 16 years they are a working master; the last decade they teach. The whole society organizes around this fact.

physicalNotes: Average height 5'8". Hands disproportionately large for their frame; childhood apprentice work shapes the bones. Calloused before puberty.

racialFeatures.label: Born to one craft
racialFeatures.description: Each human can only ever be a master of one trade. The trade is decided by birth district + family lineage and is essentially impossible to escape. Cross-training produces a competent practitioner who is a poor master of either trade.

racialFeatures.label: Early mastery, early end
racialFeatures.description: Apprenticeship starts at 7. By 14 they are journeymen; by 21 they are masters; by 35 they are teachers; by 50 most are dead. The compression makes them very, very good at their one thing for a short, intense window.

racialFeatures.label: Succession anxiety
racialFeatures.description: Because a craft dies with its master, every human is preoccupied with who will succeed them. This shapes their politics, their marriages, and their fights.

subPopulations.label: Threadwardens
subPopulations.summary: The weaving families. The city is named for them. Their looms are the largest in the world. They name their cloth.

subPopulations.label: Coldhammers
subPopulations.summary: The smiths. They forge cold whenever they can — heated metal cools wrong on Threadwarden's particular humid air, they say. The other races don't believe them. They are still the best smiths in the world.

subPopulations.label: Furrowfolk
subPopulations.summary: Grain farmers. Single crop per family — wheat OR barley OR rye, never two. Furrowmouth is their market town.

subPopulations.label: Pageborn
subPopulations.summary: Scribes. The only humans who learn another tongue (always orcish — paper comes from the orcs). Considered slightly suspect by the other sub-populations for it.

voice: direct, time-pressured, generous with compliments to apprentices and stingy with them to peers.
